---
title: "Phase II: Virtual Reality & Imputation"
---

```r
#| label: load-state
#| echo: false
# Load variables from previous phase
load("pva_state.RData")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(jagsUI)
```

Real-world data is messy. Here we generate synthetic data across three monitoring beaches and purposefully inject 30% missing data, which we will impute via a Bayesian state-space model.

```r
#| label: mock-data-and-jags
#| code-summary: "Generate Synthetic Data & Run JAGS Imputation"
#| results: hide

# ====================================================================
# 2. Synthetic historical data generation matrix (making fake data)
# ====================================================================
message(">>> Constructing data matrices for synthetic portfolio evaluation...")

# Simulate tracking data lines across three sample monitoring nesting beaches, Alpha, Beta, Gamma
mock_abundance <- expand.grid(Year = hist_years, Site = c("Alpha_Beach", "Beta_Beach", "Gamma_Beach")) %>%
  mutate(
    True_Females = ifelse(Site == "Alpha_Beach", 450, ifelse(Site == "Beta_Beach", 280, 180)) * exp(-0.025 * (Year - 2000)) * runif(n(), 0.75, 1.25),
    Annual_Nests = round(True_Females * sp_clutch_freq)
  )

# Simulate fishery observer length and mortality measurements
mock_observer <- data.frame(
  Year = sort(sample(hist_years[hist_years >= 2005], 50, replace = TRUE)),
  Species = "Mock_Sea_Turtle",
  SCL_raw = round(rnorm(50, mean = 76.5, sd = 9.8), 1),
  M_mu = round(runif(50, min = 0.25, max = 0.75), 3)
)

# Simulate total fleet-wide interaction estimates (SAFE compliance accounts)
mock_safe <- data.frame(Year = hist_years) %>%
  mutate(Total_Est = ifelse(Year < 2005, 0, round(rnorm(n(), mean = 22, sd = 3))))

# ==============================================================================
# 2.5 Bayesian Seasonal Imputation of Missing Monthly Data
# ==============================================================================
message(">>> Generating monthly seasonal data, injecting gaps, and running JAGS imputation...")

# 1. Create a monthly dataset with a simulated seasonal wave (peak in summer)
months_total <- n_hist_yrs * 12
mock_monthly <- expand.grid(
  Month_Idx = 1:months_total,
  Site = c("Alpha_Beach", "Beta_Beach", "Gamma_Beach")
) %>%
  mutate(
    # Derive Year and Month mathematically so it maps perfectly to the 756 rows
    Year = min(hist_years) + floor((Month_Idx - 1) / 12),
    Month = (Month_Idx - 1) %% 12 + 1,

    # Base annual trend
    True_Base = ifelse(Site == "Alpha_Beach", 450,
                       ifelse(Site == "Beta_Beach", 280, 180)) * exp(-0.025 * (Year - min(hist_years))),

    # Add trigonometric seasonality: strong peak around month 6/7
    Seasonal_Multiplier = pmax(0.1, 1 + 0.8 * cos(2 * pi * (Month - 6.5) / 12)),
    True_Nests = (True_Base / 12) * Seasonal_Multiplier * runif(n(), 0.8, 1.2),

    # Poke holes to create missing data (30% missing)
    Observed_Nests = ifelse(runif(n()) < 0.30, NA, round(True_Nests))
  )

# Reshape for JAGS (Sites as rows, Months as columns)
obs_matrix <- mock_monthly %>%
  select(Month_Idx, Site, Observed_Nests) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Month_Idx, values_from = Observed_Nests) %>%
  select(-Site) %>%
  as.matrix()

# 2. Define the JAGS state-space model with seasonal harmonics
jags_seasonal_model <- "
model {
  for (i in 1:n_sites) {
    # Initial state
    X[i, 1] ~ dnorm(mu0, tau0)

    # Site-specific seasonal parameters
    beta_cos[i] ~ dnorm(0, 0.01)
    beta_sin[i] ~ dnorm(0, 0.01)

    for (t in 1:n_months) {
      # Trigonometric seasonal component
      seasonality[i, t] <- beta_cos[i] * cos(2 * pi * month_seq[t] / period) +
                           beta_sin[i] * sin(2 * pi * month_seq[t] / period)

      # Combined expected mean (underlying state + seasonality)
      mu_obs[i, t] <- X[i, t] + seasonality[i, t]
      Y[i, t] ~ dnorm(mu_obs[i, t], tau_obs)
    }

    # Random walk for the underlying annual trend
    for (t in 2:n_months) {
      X[i, t] ~ dnorm(X[i, t-1] + u, tau_proc)
    }
  }

  # Global Priors
  mu0 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  tau0 <- pow(sigma0, -2)
  sigma0 ~ dunif(0, 100)

  u ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  tau_proc <- pow(sigma_proc, -2)
  sigma_proc ~ dunif(0, 100)

  tau_obs <- pow(sigma_obs, -2)
  sigma_obs ~ dunif(0, 100)
}
"

# 3. Bundle the monthly data
jags_data <- list(
  Y = log(obs_matrix + 1), # Log-transform to stabilize variance
  n_sites = nrow(obs_matrix),
  n_months = ncol(obs_matrix),
  month_seq = rep(1:12, times = n_hist_yrs),
  period = 12, # 12 months in a standard annual nesting cycle
  pi = pi
)

# Monitor the combined mean (mu_obs) to extract our imputed values
jags_params <- c("mu_obs", "beta_cos", "beta_sin", "u")

# 4. Run JAGS

# Write the model string to a temporary file so parallel workers can read it
model_file_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".txt")
writeLines(jags_seasonal_model, con = model_file_path)

out_jags <- jags(
  data = jags_data,
  parameters.to.save = jags_params,
  model.file = model_file_path, # Pass the file path instead of textConnection
  n.chains = 3,
  n.iter = 5000,
  n.burnin = 1000,
  n.thin = 2,
  parallel = TRUE,
  verbose = FALSE
)

# Clean up the temporary file afterward
unlink(model_file_path)

# 5. Extract the median imputed values
imputed_log_matrix <- out_jags$q50$mu_obs
imputed_nests <- exp(imputed_log_matrix) - 1

# 6. Merge imputed values and aggregate back into annual totals for the PVA
mock_abundance <- mock_monthly %>%
  arrange(Site, Month_Idx) %>%
  mutate(
    Imputed_Nests = pmax(0, as.vector(t(imputed_nests))), # Ensure no negative nests
    Final_Monthly_Nests = ifelse(is.na(Observed_Nests), Imputed_Nests, Observed_Nests)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Year, Site) %>%
  summarise(
    Annual_Nests = sum(Final_Monthly_Nests),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

message(">>> Seasonal imputation complete. Annual totals reconstructed for PVA.")
```

```r
#| label: trend-plot
#| code-fold: false
#| fig-cap: "Manuscript Fig 1: Baseline Trend Fit across simulated nesting beaches."

# ===============================================
# 3. Baseline trend extraction from nesting data
# ===============================================
message(">>> Simulating state-space posterior variance structures...")

# extracts the background population growth rate (U) and process variance (Q)
mock_posteriors <- list(
  U = rnorm(n_sims, mean = -0.028, sd = 0.004), # Regional trend posterior distribution
  Q = rgamma(n_sims, shape = 2, rate = 45)      # Process noise variance distribution
)
start_pop_draws <- rnorm(n_sims, mean = 720, sd = 45) * sp_remig_int

# --- Manuscript figure, Trend Fit with three nesting beaches ---
p_fit <- ggplot(mock_abundance, aes(x = Year, y = log(Annual_Nests/sp_clutch_freq), color = Site)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(y = "log(Annual Nesting Cohort)")

print(p_fit)
ggsave(filename = paste0(out_dir, "/Manuscript_Fig1_Trend_Fit.png"), plot = p_fit, width = 7, height = 4.5)

save.image("pva_state.RData")
```
